sections.title: Starting off
Veronica during quest

lines.speaker: Veronica
lines.text: Have you found my sweetheart yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: Veronica
lines.text: Can you please help me? I'm in a terrible spot of trouble.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aha, sounds like a quest. I'll help.

lines.speaker: Veronica
lines.text: Yes yes, I suppose it is a quest. My fiance Ernest and I came upon this house.

lines.speaker: Veronica
lines.text: Seeing as we were a little lost Ernest decided to go in and ask for directions.

lines.speaker: Veronica
lines.text: That was an hour ago. That house looks spooky, can you go and see if you can find him for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Veronica
lines.text: Thank you, thank you. I'm very grateful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm looking for something to kill.

lines.speaker: Veronica
lines.text: Oooh, you violent person you.

sections.title: The Mad Scientist
Veronica during quest after finding out about Ernest

lines.speaker: Veronica
lines.text: Have you found my sweetheart yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, he's a chicken.

lines.speaker: Veronica
lines.text: I know he's not exactly brave but I think you're being a bit harsh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No no, he's been turned into an actual chicken by a mad scientist.

lines.speaker: Veronica
lines.text: Eeeeeek!

lines.speaker: Veronica
lines.text: My poor darling, why must these things happen to us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'm doing my best to turn him back.

lines.speaker: Veronica
lines.text: Well be quick, I'm sure being a chicken can't be good for him.

sections.title: Before returning any parts

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein
lines.text: Have you found anything yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't have any of them yet!

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein
lines.text: I need a rubber tube, a pressure gauge and a can of oil. Then your friend can stop being a chicken.

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein
lines.text: Be careful in here, there's lots of dangerous equipment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a guy called Ernest.

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein
lines.text: Ah Ernest, top notch bloke. He's helping me with my experiments.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you know where he is then?

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein
lines.text: He's that chicken over there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ernest is a chicken...? Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein
lines.text: Oh, he isn't normally a chicken, or at least he wasn't until he helped me test my pouletmorph machine.

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein
lines.text: It was originally going to be called a transmutation machine. But after testing pouletmorph seems more appropriate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm glad Veronica didn't actually get engaged to a chicken.

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein
lines.text: Who's Veronica?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ernest's fiancee. She probably doesn't want to marry a chicken.

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein
lines.text: Ooh I dunno. She could have free eggs for breakfast.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think you'd better change him back.

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein
lines.text: Umm... It's not so easy...

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein
lines.text: My machine is broken, and the house gremlins have run off with some vital bits.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I can look for them.

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein
lines.text: That would be a help. They'll be somewhere in the manor house or its grounds, the gremlins never get further than the entrance gate.

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein
lines.text: I'm missing the pressure gauge and a rubber tube. They've also taken my oil can, which I'm going to need to get this thing started again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Change him back this instant!

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein
lines.text: Umm... It's not so easy...

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein
lines.text: My machine is broken, and the house gremlins have run off with some vital bits.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I can look for them.

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein
lines.text: That would be a help. They'll be somewhere in the manor house or its grounds, the gremlins never get further than the entrance gate.

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein
lines.text: I'm missing the pressure gauge and a rubber tube. They've also taken my oil can, which I'm going to need to get this thing started again.

sections.title: The pressure gauge
Trying to search the fountain without poisoning the piranhas

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There seems to be a pressure gauge in here...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ow!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and a lot of piranhas! I can't get the gauge out.

sections.title: Using poison on the fish food
Using poisoned food on the fountain
Searching the fountain after piranhas have been poisoned

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There seems to be a pressure gauge in here...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and a lot of dead fish.

sections.title: Searching the fountain after obtaining pressure gauge

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's full of dead fish!

sections.title: Using regular fish food on the fountain
Using anything besides the fish food variants on the fountain

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ow!

sections.title: Attempting to fill a water container after poisoning the water

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think I want to use that water any more.

sections.title: The key
Returning the parts
The rubber tube

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein
lines.text: Have you found anything yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've only got the rubber tube so far.

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein
lines.text: It's a good start, but I'll need my pressure gauge and oil can too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll go and look for them.

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll go and look for it.

lines.speaker: Player

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein

lines.speaker: Player

sections.title: Finishing up

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein
lines.text: Have you found anything yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have everything!

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein
lines.text: Give 'em here then.

lines.speaker: Professor Oddenstein
lines.text: Let's get this fixed then.

lines.speaker: Ernest
lines.text: [Thank you sir/Thank you m'lady/Thank you]. It was dreadfully irritating being a chicken. How can I ever thank you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well a cash reward is always nice...

lines.speaker: Ernest
lines.text: Of course, of course.
